title: Every stynx HTTP endpoint must be either role-bound or explicitly public
statement: For every endpoint id in .devai/state/sensors/inventory_api/api-map.json#/endpoints[*], the endpoint MUST satisfy at least one of: (a) appear in .devai/state/sensors/inventory_rbac/rbac.json#/bindings/endpointBindings[*] under a non-empty permission/role binding; or (b) carry an explicit `auth.required = false` claim in the api-map entry; or (c) match an explicit allow-list of bootstrap/dev-only paths declared in docs/architecture/invariants/INV-RBAC-001-allowlist.json. The default policy is deny: an endpoint with no role binding, no public marker, and no allow-list entry is a release-gating finding, not an implicit grant.
rationale: Stynx-side specialization of DEVAI's INV-INVENTORY-003 (universal policy). Phase A of C-4 surfaced three currently-violating endpoints — GET /_reference/auth-verify, POST /_reference/dev-login, GET /_reference/demo-tenants — all in the reference-api dev-auth sample. These are legitimate public/dev-only endpoints; closure requires marking them public via api-map's `auth.required=false` (or moving them to the allow-list) rather than guarding them. The 'gate' severity reflects that any non-allow-listed unbound endpoint blocks release. Promoted from INV-CANDIDATE-{01KRQD0PQW2M64XGKB2QDD3HZM, 01KRQD0PQWAX38EH0XVJHDAMPJ, 01KRQD0PQWKZ8QD7SVAJSATX7T}.
